name: Akhnas ibn Marthad
title: Trampler of the Imam's Body
shortDescription: One of the criminals who, after Imam Hussain's (AS) martyrdom, trampled his holy body with horses on Umar Sa'd's orders, crushing his chest bones.
fullStory:
Akhnas ibn Marthad was one of the vile forces in Umar ibn Sa'd's army who committed one of the most heinous crimes in history for worldly rewards.

On the afternoon of Ashura, after Imam Hussain (AS) was martyred, Umar ibn Sa'd shouted among the army: 'Who volunteers to trample Hussain's body with horses?' Ten shameless and hard-hearted horsemen volunteered, one of whom was Akhnas ibn Marthad.

They put new horseshoes on their horses and trampled over the headless and torn body of the Master of Martyrs, crushing the bones of his chest and back. This crime is one of the most painful scenes of Ashura that pains any human heart.

After Ashura, Akhnas went to Ibn Ziyad for a reward and proudly recounted his crime, but Ibn Ziyad gave him a meager reward. Eventually, during Mukhtar's uprising, he was captured. Mukhtar ordered him to be pegged to the ground and had horses trample his body until his bones were crushed and he died.
quoteArabic: نَحْنُ رَضَضْنا الصَّدْرَ بَعْدَ الظَّهْرِ... بِكُلِّ يَعْبُوبٍ شَدِيدِ الأَسْرِ...
quote: (His poem in Ibn Ziyad's court:) We are the ones who crushed Hussain's chest and back with strong and massive horses.
